channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Supreme Court will decide if Florida and Texas are violating social media First Amendment rights
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court is expected on Monday to rule on the legality of Republican-backed laws in Florida and Texas intended to restrain social media companies from curbing content the platforms deem objectionable — statutes the industry has argued violate the free speech rights of these businesses.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: In the AI era, data is gold. And these companies are striking it rich
item.description: As businesses scramble to take advantage of artificial intelligence, they’re finding data makes all the difference in using AI effectively.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Software stocks may be primed for a big Wall Street comeback
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. It’s been a rough year for software stocks, and a very good year for hardware names such as Nvidia, which just hit a new all-time high, briefly, last week. Maybe it’s time to switch.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: LockBit ransomware attack stole data on millions of Infosys McCamish users
item.description: At first, IMS thought 57,000 people were affected by the cyberattack - but the reality is much worse.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at barring social media companies from banning users based on their political views, kicking the major First Amendment challenge down the road.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: How to trace and block anonymous calls
item.description: Do you want to prevent scam calls? Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how to block hidden IDs on your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alibaba to cease data center operations in India and Australia
item.description: Alibaba’s cloud computing unit, otherwise known as Alibaba Cloud or Aliyun, has decided to cease data center operations in India and Australia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How smaller businesses can reimagine brand building with generative AI
item.description: Generating and maintaining awareness is notoriously challenging for small businesses, often working with limited in-house resources and without the budget for external marketing support.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What key features underpin best-in-class AI PCs?
item.description: As businesses seek to leverage AI in all aspects of their operations, it’s vital that IT leaders understand how this transformative moment will impact hardware. CIOs who aren’t actively investigating the possibilities risk being left behind.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Autonomous trash-gobbling robot boat wages war on waterway waste
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides the details on the Class 3 Clearbot: an autonomous vessel for efficient marine trash/plant removal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 AI-powered apps that’ll actually save you time
item.description: You can’t open your eyes these days without seeing something about generative AI and all the reasons it’s, like, totally gonna revolutionize the way you work. And yet, call me surly, but most of the AI tools out there at this point seem far less impressive in practice than they do on paper.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cities most targeted for travel booking scams
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a look at the five travel destinations most frequently targeted by cybercriminals through online booking scams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: TSA launches 3D scanners to detect explosives
item.description: The new technology will give agents a 3D image that can be turned 360 degrees for enhanced threat protection.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: FCC Seeks to Mandate Carrier-Unlocking for All Cellphones
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission proposes a new rule requiring US carriers to unlock mobile phones within 60 days of purchase, allowing consumers greater freedom in choosing networks
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: Too many passwords to remember? Here's what you can do
item.description: According to some unscientific studies, the average person has hundreds of passwords. That's a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Tech's Eroding Trust in AI: Is Generative AI a Productivity Game-Changer or Resource-Consuming Menace?
item.description: As big tech companies invest heavily in generative AI, concerns rise about its impact on trust and resource usage. Is this revolutionary technology a productivity powerhouse or an intrusive waste of vital resources?
item.dc:creator: Irina Raicu, The Mercury News

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: White House to hold conference for digital creators
item.description: The Biden administration will host digital creators at the White House for the first-ever Creator Economy Conference in August, the director of digital strategy announced Friday. The conference, which will be held Aug.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google opens access to 2 million context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro
item.description: Google has opened access to the 2-million-token context window of the Gemini 1.5 Pro AI model. The company also is giving developers access to code execution capabilities in the Gemini API and making the Gemma 2 model available in Google AI Studio. These announcements were made on June 27.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vestager Criticizes Apple's Delayed AI Rollout Amid Antitrust Concerns
item.description: European Commission Vice President Margrethe Vestager has condemned Apple's decision to delay introducing its AI technology, accusing the company of displaying 'stunning' anticompetitive behavior.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: Android smartphones for business
item.description: Android dominates smartphone usage throughout the world — in every region except North America and Oceania. Thus, businesses in many regions are likely to support and issue Android devices to employees as their mainstay mobile devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Doxxing victims in California may gain right to sue to ‘bring some power back’
item.description: A state bill would allow doxxing victims to sue those responsible in civil court for as much as $30,000, plus attorneys’ fees and court costs. Supporters say it’s needed to stem online harassment against LGBTQ residents and others.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Separating Fact from Fiction in AI Success Stories
item.description: Discovering the truth behind AI's impressive achievements, separating reality from hype and exploring how excessive enthusiasm may hinder the technology's actual progress.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised its national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia Fends Off Ransomware Attack, Refuses to Pay $8 Million Demand
item.description: Jakarta, Indonesia - A sophisticated cyberattack has compromised the country's national data center, prompting a hacking group to demand an $8 million ransom. However, the government has refused to pay the hefty sum and is instead working to contain the breach.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car dealerships in North America revert to pens and paper after cyberattacks on software provider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Car dealerships in North America are still wrestling with major disruptions that started last week with cyberattacks on a company whose software is used widely in the auto retail sales sector. CDK Global, a company that provides software for thousands of auto dealers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UN launches global principles to combat online hate and demands big tech take action now
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief on Monday launched global principles to combat online hate and lies and demanded that big tech companies use their power to reduce the harm they are doing to people and societies around the world.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Music record labels sue AI song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Big record companies are suing artificial intelligence song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement, alleging that the AI music startups are exploiting the recorded works of artists from Chuck Berry to Mariah Carey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple becomes first target of EU's new digital competition rules aimed at big tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators on Monday leveled their first charges under the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook, accusing Apple of preventing app makers from pointing users to cheaper options outside its App Store.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
